title: MiniMobToys
description: Your Favorite Mobs, as Miniature Toys!
body:
![Miniature Toys!](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f98c93b1c87b9ff2fbf7e62c5fb1b51821728fc6_0.webp)

# **IMPORTANT** - I (AverageTaTeMan) only share my content on [Planet Minecraft](https://www.planetminecraft.com/member/averagetateman/), [Modrinth](https://modrinth.com/user/AverageTaTeMan), and [CurseForge](https://www.curseforge.com/members/averagetateman/projects) - If you see this project on any other site, IT IS NOT ME AND SHOULD NOT BE TRUSTED!

Ever wished you could display the creatures of Minecraft in the form of harmless toys? This pack makes that wish come true! Toys can be made in the Crafting Table using an Armor Stand, Sculk Catalyst, and two mob appropriate items!

<details>
<summary>Manual (Can also be found in pack folder and in-game)</summary>
  
**In-Game Manual:**
-   To get help in-game, you can craft a book that has many of these instructions using a Book and Quill, a Sculk Catalyst, and an Armor Stand.

**Creation:**
- 	Craft a toy using an Armor Stand, Sculk Catalyst and 2 mob specific items.
- 	Place it down, and the mob toy will soon appear!

**Deletion:**
- 	Craft a Recall Hammer using Copper Ingots, Blaze Rods, and a Sculk Catalyst.
- 	Hit the toy you wish to remove, then you will get the toy item back.

**Equipment:**
- 	Many toys can be equipped with armor and other listed items by hitting them with the item.
- 	To remove any equipment, hit it with Shears/use Shears on it.

**Age Control:**
- 	A toy can be turned into its baby variant (if it has one) by hitting it with a Clock.
- 	Hit it again to grow it back up.

**Sound Control:**
- 	A toy can be silenced by hitting it with a Sculk Sensor.
- 	Hit it again to unsilence it.

**Hand Control:**
- 	A toy can be set to be left-handed by hitting it with a Crimson/Warped Fungus.
- 	Hit it again to make it right-handed.

**Rotation Control:**
- 	Change a toy's horizontal rotation (yaw) by hitting it with Magenta Glazed Terracotta.
- 	Change a toy's vertical rotation (pitch) by hitting it with Ladders.

**Fire Control:**
- 	A toy can be set on fire (if not resistant to it) by hitting it with a Lava Bucket.
- 	Hit it again to put out the fire.

**Freeze Control:**
- 	A toy can be made to freeze (if not resistant to it) by hitting it with a Powder Snow Bucket.
- 	Hit it again to make it stop freezing.

**Invisibility Control:**
- 	A toy can be made invisible by hitting it with clear Glass/Glass Panes.
- 	Hit it again to make it visible.

**Glowing Control:**
- 	A toy can be set to glow by hitting it with a Spectral Arrow.
- 	Hit it again to make it stop glowing.

**Watcher Control:**
- 	A toy can be set to watch the nearest player in a certain range by hitting it with an Eye of Ender.
- 	Hit it again to make it stop watching.

**Invulnerability Control:**
- 	A toy can be made invulnerable by hitting it with a Heavy Core.
- 	Hit it again to make it vulnerable.

**Scale Control:**
- 	A toy's scale can be changed by hitting it with an Armor Stand.
- 	Scales: Atom (0.0625), Ant (0.125), Mini (0.25, Default), Half (0.5), Normal (1), Double (2), Large (4), Jumbo (8), Titan (16)

**Waypoint Control:**
- 	A toy can be made into a waypoint by hitting it with a Beacon.
- 	Hit it again to remove the waypoint.

**Color Control:**
- 	A toy's color can be changed by hitting it with Tinted Glass, affecting its name, waypoint, and outline when glowing.
- 	List of Colors: White, Aqua, Black, Blue, Dark Aqua, Dark Blue, Dark Gray, Dark Green, Dark Purple, Dark Red, Gold, Gray, Green, Light Purple, Red, Yellow

**Mount Control:**
- 	A toy can be set to be a passenger by hitting it with a Saddle while not sneaking.
- 	Hit it again while not sneaking to remove its status as a passenger.
- 	A toy can be set to be a vehicle by hitting it with a Saddle while sneaking.
- 	Hit it again while sneaking to remove its status as a vehicle.

**Mob Specific Help:**
- 	Hit a toy with the Manual to see its information and instructions.
- 	Unless a default interaction exists or specified otherwise, the way to change toys is by hitting it with the specified items.

**Mob Specific Items/Abilities:**

	Allay:
		-To Create: 2 Amethyst Shards
		-Unique Equips: Any Item
	
	Armadillo:
		-To Create: 2 Armadillo Scutes
		-Make Idle/Scared: Spider Eye

	Axolotl:
		-To Create: 1 Moss Block + 1 Tropical Fish Bucket
		-Change Variant: Appropriate Axolotl Bucket

	Bat:
		-To Create: 2 Glow Berries
		-Make Hang/Fly: Glow Berries

	Bee:
		-To Create: 2 Honeycomb
		-Make Angry/Calm: Glass Bottle
		-Add/Remove Nectar: Sunflower
		-Add/Remove Stinger: Wither Rose

	Blaze:
		-To Create: 2 Blaze Rods

	Bogged:
		-To Create: 1 Bone + 1 Mushroom
		-Remove Mushrooms: Shears
	
	Breeze:
		-To Create: 2 Breeze Rods
	
	Camel:
		-To Create: 2 Cacti
		-Unique Equips: Saddle
		-Make Lay/Stand: Cactus

	Camel Husk:
		-To Create: 1 Rotten Flesh + 1 Rabbit Foot
		-Unique Equips: Saddle
		-Make Lay/Stand: Cactus

	Cat:
		-To Create: 1 Raw Cod + 1 String
		-Make White: White Wool
		-Make Tuxedo: Green Wool
		-Make Ginger: Pumpkin
		-Make Siamese: Light Blue Wool
		-Make British Shorthair: Gray Wool
		-Make Calico: Carved Pumpkin
		-Make Persian: Sand
		-Make Ragdoll: Light Gray Wool
		-Make Tabby: Brown Wool
		-Make Black: Black Wool
		-Make Jellie: Dirt
		-Tame: Raw Cod/Salmon, 
		-Change Collar Color: Dye

	Chicken:

		-Make Brown: Dirt
		-Make Gray: Stone

	Magma Cube:
		-To Create: 2 Magma Cream
		-Change Size: Magma Cream

	Mannequin:
		-To Create: 1 Head + 1 Redstone
		-Change Profile/Name/Texture:
			-Hit with unnamed Head to switch between Mannequin/Player Profile Type.
			-Hit Mannequin Profile Type Mannequin with named Head to set the profile texture to the item's name (texture must be in /assets/minecraft/textures/entity/player).
			-Hit Player Profile Type Mannequin with named Head to set the profile name to the item's name.
		-Change Cape Texture:
			-Hit with named Wool to set the Cape texture to the item's name (texture must be in /assets/minecraft/textures/entity/equipment/cape)
			-Hit with unnamed Wool to remove the texture.
		-Change Elytra Texture:
			-Hit with named Elytra to set the Elytra texture to the item's name (texture must be in /assets/minecraft/textures/entity/equipment/wings)
			-Hit with unnamed Elytra to remove the texture.
		-Change Model: Cake
		-Change Hidden Layers:
			-Hit with named Leather to hide the layer listed in the item's name (Valid Values: hat, jacket, left_sleeve, right_sleeve, left_pants_leg, right_pants_leg, cape).
			-Hit with unnamed Leather to show all layers.
		-Change Pose: Piston
		-Change Description:
			-Hit Named Mannequin with a named Goat Horn to set its description to the item's name.
			-Hit with an unnamed Goat Horn to remove the description.
		-Change Immovable: Netherite Ingot
		-Tries to Pick up Dropped Blocks and Items in a Unique Order:
			-Head
			-Chest
			-Legs
			-Feet
			-Mainhand
			-Offhand

**Installation Instructions to Get the Data AND Resource Packs:**
1) Click "Versions"
![Click "Versions"](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0fa5986659f549bce8d54fc7fa0182ce7c9cee0f.png)

2) Select a Version
![Select a Version](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/786593f6790747dfe34d64f1f31ba6fe90646678.png)

3) Under "Files", click "Download" on both the 'Primary' file (data pack) and 'Required resource pack' file (resource pack)
![Under "Files", click "Download" on both the 'Primary' file (data pack) and 'Required resource pack' file (resource pack)](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/9dbfcbe10de4a5520daa540e68d03397f930291f.png)

4) Unzip the files (optional, but recommended as that's how I use/test them). 

5) Add the resource pack to your Selected Packs (ideally at the top) in your Resource Packs menu. 

6) Add the data pack to either your Selected Packs in the Data Packs menu (if adding at world creation) OR put it in your world's 'datapacks' folder (if adding afterwards.  Recommended to either not have world open at the time or to close and reopen it afterwards).

7) You may get a prompt about experimental settings not being supported when opening the world.  This can safely be ignored, but it's also never a bad idea to make a backup if it's a world you care about.
